structured.supersededBy: agenticstory skills: add-prop, add-motif, add-visual-metaphor, lock-references

prose.rule: SUPERSEDED BY THE FRAMEWORK. Element art is scaffolded with the agenticstory `add-prop` / `add-motif` / `add-visual-metaphor` skills and locked with `lock-references`, which already does isolated generation, read-back against declared invariants, and per-shot locking via `agenticstory lock-shot`. Do not hand-write element entities and do not hand-roll generation.
prose.whatSurvives: Only the vocabulary. Elements are named for what they MEAN, not what they depict: `startup-stack`, not `three blocks`. An element whose meaning cannot be stated in one sentence is not an element yet, it is a scene.
prose.whyThisRecordExists: Kept as a tombstone rather than deleted, because the mistake is worth remembering. Two lessons were paid for before the framework was consulted. First: generating a composite asks the model to re-decide everything already settled, so one note on one element cost a full re-roll of all four. Second: the register anchor was null the entire time, and `lock-references` would have refused to generate at all. The drift was not bad luck, it was an ungated pipeline.
prose.source: Gary, 2026-07-22: 'we should use the /agenticstory plugin skills ya?'
